task_completion.description: The target image is opened in an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screenshot shows an image editing application (like GIMP) with the target image loaded in the workspace.

task_completion.description: A tool for removing background or converting color to alpha is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or tool settings panel for 'Color to Alpha', 'Fuzzy Select', 'Magic Wand', or background removal is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The color white is selected as the target color to be removed or made transparent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the transparency or selection tool settings, the color white (#FFFFFF) is explicitly selected as the target color to be converted to alpha/removed.

task_completion.description: The white background is successfully removed, revealing transparency.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the workspace now shows a checkerboard pattern (the standard visual indicator for transparency in image editors) where the white background used to be.

task_completion.description: The modified image is exported or saved in a format that supports transparency (PNG).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog is visible, showing the file being saved with a '.png' extension, which is required to preserve the alpha channel.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of an image editor rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open executing ImageMagick commands (e.g., 'convert', 'mogrify', 'magick') to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine image editing application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application used must be a full image editor (like GIMP) with toolbars and layers, not just a basic image viewer or a web-based mock tool.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download a pre-made transparent image from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no web browser open searching for or downloading a replacement PNG image with a transparent background.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is a JPEG (which does not support transparency) with a white background. To succeed, the white background must be removed (at least 30% white pixel reduction) to create transparency (at least 15% of the image). The final image must be exported in PNG format to preserve the alpha channel. Visually, transparency is indicated by a checkerboard pattern in the image editor workspace.
